WINNING RESULT #453 (2026-04-15 18:25) + cycle 30 CORS RESOLUTION. Frontend-inspect captured complete SSE wire format: api.individual.githubcopilot.com/github/chat/threads/{threadId}/messages POST returns text/event-stream with 2-event-type schema (content + complete) — SIMPLER than DeepSeek's JSON-Patch. Phase 4: Option A SSE injection HIGHLY VIABLE; B/C/D/E all blocked or marginal. Error UI is static-i18n primer-react Banner confirmed via fetch override + empty 500 body. Phase 5 design.md complete with full SQL migration: INSERT new copilot_sse envelope template + UPDATE ai_prompt_services + 2x revision_cnt bumps. **Cycle 30 RESOLVED the CORS open question**: Read ai_prompt_filter.cpp render_envelope_template (lines 1249-1328) and generate_block_response (lines 1602-1677), confirmed APF synthesizes response from envelope_template verbatim — no upstream header preservation, no stripping. CORS headers embedded in the new copilot_sse template (Access-Control-Allow-Origin: https://github.com + Allow-Credentials + Expose-Headers) will render into the wire response exactly as written. NO C++ CHANGE REQUIRED. Root cause of existing copilot_403 failure diagnosed: old template lacks CORS headers, cross-origin browser rejects the 403 before React sees it. Phase 6 is now pure DB-only migration matching deepseek+v0 pattern. Body sizing ~710B fits under h2_end_stream=1 ceiling. Joins deepseek + v0 in phase6_db_blocked bucket — three services queue for the same DB window at 218.232.120.58, combined transaction ~25-30min.
